questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a stem-and-leaf plot in healthcare statistics?

options.B: To display the distribution of patient data
options.D: To identify trends in cell growth

questions.question: A researcher collects data on the $\number\ of\ hours$ patients spend in the hospital. The stem-and-leaf plot for this data shows stems of $2$, $3$, and $4$. What can be inferred about the data?

options.A: Most patients spend less than $20$ hours in the hospital
options.B: Most patients spend between $20$ and $30$ hours in the hospital
options.C: Most patients spend between $30$ and $40$ hours in the hospital
options.D: Most patients spend more than $40$ hours in the hospital

questions.question: Which of the following is a benefit of using a stem-and-leaf plot to display data on $\milligrams\ of\ medication$?

options.A: It allows for easy calculation of mean and median
options.B: It provides a clear visual representation of the data distribution
options.C: It enables identification of outliers and skewness
options.D: It facilitates comparison with other datasets

questions.question: A stem-and-leaf plot shows the following data: $\{3|1, 2, 4, 5, 7, 9\}$. What is the $\median$ value of this dataset?
Which type of data is best suited for a stem-and-leaf plot?

options.B: Numerical data with a small range
options.C: Numerical data with a large range

questions.question: What does the 'stem' in a stem-and-leaf plot typically represent?

options.A: The most significant digit of the data value
options.B: The least significant digit of the data value
options.C: The median of the data values
options.D: The standard deviation of the data values
options.E: The range of the data values

questions.question: A researcher wants to compare the distribution of $\blood\ pressure$ values between two patient groups. Which of the following is a suitable method?

options.B: Create a single stem-and-leaf plot with different colors for each group
options.C: Calculate the mean and standard deviation for each group
options.D: Use a histogram to display the data
options.E: Use a box plot to display the data

questions.question: What can be inferred about a dataset with a stem-and-leaf plot that shows a large gap between the stems?

options.B: The data is skewed to the left
options.C: The data is skewed to the right
options.D: The data has a bimodal distribution

questions.question: Create a stem-and-leaf plot for the following data on $\number\ of\ patients$ treated per day: $12, 15, 18, 20, 22, 25, 30, 32, 35, 38, 40, 42$. What can be inferred about the distribution of this data? Provide at least $3$ observations.

A stem-and-leaf plot shows the following data on $\levels\ of\ medication$ ($\milligrams$): $\{2|0, 2, 5, 8\}$, $\{3|1, 4, 6, 9\}$. Calculate the $\mean$, $\median$, and $\mode$ of this dataset. Provide your work and explain the significance of each value.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Ensure students understand the purpose of stem-and-leaf plots in data analysis
Tip 2: Encourage students to practice creating and interpreting stem-and-leaf plots with different types of data
Tip 3: Remind students to consider the context and limitations of the data when making inferences from a stem-and-leaf plot
